Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]

{{神社 |名称 = 潮江天満宮 |画像 = [[画像:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[高知市]]天神町19番20号 |位置 = {{Coord|33|33|11.5|N|133|32|4.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[菅原道真]]公
[[菅原高視|高視朝臣]]
北の御方 |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[県社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[延喜]]5年（[[905年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月22日]]（夏祭） |神事 = 春大祭（古式菜種祭）（[[2月25日]]）
秋大祭（神幸祭）（[[11月18日]]・[[11月19日|19日]]） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=潮江天満宮|coordinates= {{coord2|33|33|11.5|N|133|32|4.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|33|11.5|N|133|32|4.2|E}}}} }} '''潮江天満宮'''（うしおえてんまんぐう）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]天神町にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[別表神社]]。 「潮江天満宮」は通称であり、正式名称は「天満宮」。市南半分の産土神であり『高知市史』潮江天満宮項、482-483頁。、高知市の中心部に鎮座することもあって、[[初詣]]では[[土佐国]][[一宮]]の[[土佐神社]]をしのぎ市内最多の参拝者を集める。 == 祭神 == '''主祭神''' * [[菅原道真]]公 * 高視朝臣 - 道真嫡男の[[菅原高視]]（たかみ）。 * 北の御方 '''相殿神''' * [[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]] - [[菅原氏]]の祖神。 * 大海津見神 （[[ワタツミ|大綿津見神]]に同じ） == 歴史 == === 創建 === [[昌泰]]4年（[[901年]]）の[[昌泰の変]]による菅原道真の左遷に伴い、その息子である[[大学頭]]兼[[弁官|右少弁]]菅原高視も土佐権守に任官されて左遷され、[[土佐国]]潮江に居住した。高視邸跡は昔は「小判畑」と呼ばれており、現在は石碑が建てられている。邸跡のある山は高視の名前から「高見山」（筆山の奥の山、正式名は皿ヶ峰）と呼ばれ、麓には現在も高見町という町名が残っている。 [[延喜]]3年（[[903年]]）に道真が薨去し、その御璽（御鏡、紫御袍、神息の御神剣）観音像が2年後に高視の元へ届けられたので、それらを祀ったのが創祀とされる。 なお、この際届けたのが道真公侍臣の[[渡会春彦]]（白太夫）であり、[[長岡郡]][[大津 (高知市)|大津村]]（現 高知市[[大津 (高知市)|大津]]）の霊松山雲門寺において病を発し、[[延喜]]5年（[[905年]]）[[12月9日]]高視に会うことなく同地で没したと伝わる。その後遺品は他の者により届けられた。現在も岩崎山（大津北浦）には、その墓（[[奥津城]]）が残り白太夫神社が建てられている。 その後、京で数々の異変があったこともあり、高視は[[延喜]]6年（[[906年]]）京へ戻り[[従五位|従五位上]]に叙され大学頭に復帰している。 === 概史 === 長宗我部氏の時代・宮地正勝（若左衛門）が祭祀職を務め以来、1871年（明治4年）の太政官布告によって社家が廃止されるまで、代々その子孫が神主職（宮司職）を世襲した。宮地正勝の子孫には、[[坂本龍馬]]や[[沢辺琢磨]]、[[山本信敬]]、[[宮地信貞]]、[[宮地茂春]]、[[谷干城]]らがいる。{{main|坂本龍馬の系譜}} [[江戸時代]]、[[土佐藩]]主[[土佐山内氏|山内氏]]の菩提寺（[[真如寺 (高知市)|真如寺]]）を築くにあたって廃社寸前の所、 宮地若左衛門の子息・宮地平兵衛が私財を投じて、天満宮の社殿を慶長11年9月に自力建立し再興したと言われる『土佐の宮地氏』。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）県社に列格し、明治35年（[[1902年]]）の菅原道真一千年祭にあたって大幅な修築が行われている。本殿は[[大東亜戦争]]の際に敵機の空襲に罹り焼失。戦後は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に列している。 == 境内 == 境内は[[筆山公園|筆山]]の麓、[[鏡川]]と挟まれた地に建つ。鏡川右岸に沿うようにあり、川の下流側である東側が入口となる。本殿は入口同様東面しているが、参道はS字に折れている。すなわち、参道は入口からしばらく西に向かったあと左に直角に折れ、楼門をくぐったのち今度は右に直角に折れる。境内には他の天満宮同様、多くの老梅がある。 社殿のうち楼門は、[[嘉永]]6年（[[1853年]]）の造営。1887年（明治20年）に修築されており、三間一戸[[入母屋造]]、銅板葺で、高知市指定有形文化財に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kochi.kochi.jp/soshiki/90/cas-city-3100800.html|title=文化財情報　有形文化財　潮江天満宮楼門|publisher=高知市|date=|accessdate=2022-12-13}}。 また境内左手の鏡川堤防上に立つ[[クスノキ]]は、樹高25メートル、目通り幹囲7.5メートルを測る。推定樹齢は300年以上。このクスノキは「天神のクスノキ」として高知市指定天然記念物に指定されている。また、境内は特別天然記念物[[ミカドアゲハ]]の保護のため、筆山町[[要法寺]]境内・潮江中学校校庭とともに「高知市のミカドアゲハ及びその生息地」に登録されている。 ファイル:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|飛龍梅 ファイル:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|楼門（高知市指定文化財） == 摂末社 == * 白太夫社 ** 祭神：松本春彦（渡会春彦）。祭日：[[2月25日]]、[[7月22日]]、[[11月18日]]・[[11月19日|19日]] *: 祭神は菅原道真の侍臣で、「[[菅原伝授手習鑑]]」の白太夫として著名。 * 若栄社（わかえしゃ） ** 祭神：菅公陪従の諸霊と宮地正勝（若左衛門）。祭日：2月25日、7月22日、11月18日・19日 * 早良宮 *: 元は高知市唐人町にあったが、同地に藩主[[土佐山内氏|山内氏]]の別邸を築くにあたって鷹匠町藤並神社内に遷されたのち、[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）3月当地に遷された。 * 島崎神社 ** 祭神：島崎道和（道久）。祭日：2月25日、7月22日、11月18日・19日 *: [[寛政]]6年（[[1794年]]）10月勧請。島崎道和は当社近くに住んでいた人物で、当社を篤く崇敬したため祀られた。 * 大山祇神社 ** 祭神：[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]。祭日：2月25日、7月22日、11月18日・19日。旧暦20日に月例祭。 *: [[手箱山]]より勧請。 * 幡龍宮 *: 神池のほとりに龍神が祀られている。 == 文化財 == === 高知市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 潮江天満宮楼門（建造物） - 昭和42年2月3日指定。 * 天然記念物 ** 天神町のオオクスノキ - 昭和42年2月3日指定。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[高知県]][[高知市]]天神町19番20号 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[とさでん交通]][[とさでん交通伊野線|伊野線]] [[大橋通停留場]] （南方向へ徒歩約12分） '''周辺''' * [[筆山公園|筆山]] * [[鏡川]] *: 天神大橋 - [[真如寺 (高知市)|真如寺]]の山内家廟の為に元和年間に架けられ、当初は「真如寺橋」の名であった。8代藩主[[山内豊敷|豊敷]]の代で潮江天満宮にちなみ改称。 * [[真如寺 (高知市)|真如寺]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『高知市史』（高知市役所、[[1920年]]） *『家牒』 *太田亮『姓氏家系大辞典』 *『「宮地神仙道」という幻想』（2014年） == 関連項目 == * [[安楽寺 (高知市)|安楽寺]] （高知市） - [[四国八十八箇所]]第30番札所奥の院。菅原高視による開基と伝える。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ 潮江天満宮公式ホームページ] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php 潮江天満宮と高見山] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web高知] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんくう}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:高知市の歴史]] [[Category:高知県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:高知市の建築物]]

{{Shrine |Name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|Latitude minutes=33|Latitude seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|Longitude minutes=32|Longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |annual festival=July 22 (Summer Festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand Festival (Old Style Rapeseed Festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand Festival (Shinko Festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** Deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** Deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** Deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** Deity: Oyamatsumi. Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** Deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == Location Information == '''Location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]

Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** Deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == Location Information == '''Location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. {{Shrine |Name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|Latitude minutes=33|Latitude seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|Longitude minutes=32|Longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |annual festival=July 22 (Summer Festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand Festival (Old Style Rapeseed Festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand Festival (Shinko Festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** Deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** Deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** Deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** Deity: Oyamatsumi. Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** Deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == Location Information == '''Location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:[[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine[[Category:original pages]] ]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]

Autumn Grand Festival (Shinko Festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** Deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** Deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** Deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** Deity: Oyamatsumi. Festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** Deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == Location Information == '''Location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]

In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|Longitude minutes=32|Longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |annual festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]][[category:original pages]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]][[category:original pages]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|Longitude minutes=32|Longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |annual festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]][[category:original pages]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]][[category:original pages]]

Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]][[category:original pages]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]][[category:original pages]]

In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples.
