{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905年}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905th year }}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905th year }}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905th year }}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|うしおえてんまんぐう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|12=draft}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視|12=draft}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905th year|12=draft}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|12=draft}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視|12=draft}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ|12=draft}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|12=draft}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変|12=draft}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦|12=draft}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川|12=draft}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通|12=draft}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場|12=draft}}, 12-minute walk south. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine |image=[[File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden and rope torii.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location={{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|12=draft}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|rank minutes=33|rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視|12=draft}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}
{{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|ja|905th year|12=draft}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|ja|潮江天満宮|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|12=draft}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視|12=draft}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ|12=draft}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|12=draft}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変|12=draft}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦|12=draft}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川|12=draft}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosa Electric Railway|ja|とさでん交通|12=draft}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ohashi-dori Station|ja|大橋通停留場|12=draft}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷|12=draft}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)|12=draft}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|うしおえてんまんぐう}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} {{citation needed|Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] [[Category:Tenmangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ushioe Tenmangu]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine]] [[ja:潮江天満宮]]
