Haiden |location={{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kochi City|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, Tenjin-cho 19-20 |ISO=JP-39 |latitude degrees=33|Rank minutes=33|Rank seconds=11.54 |longitude degrees=133|longitude minutes=32|longitude seconds=04.23 |deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Takamasa|1=Sugawara no Takami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619246}}, Northern Lady |divine object= |Rank=Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} |founded=Legendary: Enki 5 ({{ill|905|en|905|WD=Q24239|lt=905|1=905}}) |main hall= |other names= |pilgrimage sites= |festival=July 22 (Summer festival) |religious ceremonies=Spring Grand festival (Old Style Rapeseed festival) (February 25)
Autumn Grand festival (Shinko festival) (November 18-19) |map=Japan Kochi }} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.553206|133.534508}}|caption=Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|width=256}} '''Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine''' (うしおえてんまんぐう{{ill|Ushioe Tenmangu|lt=Ushioe Tenmangu|lt=Ushioe Tenmangu shrine|ja|潮江天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kochi City|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. The formal name is "Tenmangu." It is the guardian deity of the southern half of Kochi City and, due to its central location, attracts the most visitors in the city during {{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumode|1=Hatsumōde}}, surpassing even the Tosa Shrine, the Ichinomiya of Tosa Province. == Deities == '''Main Deities''' * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Takamasa Ason - Michizane's eldest son, {{ill|Sugawara Takamasa|ja|菅原高視|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Takamasa|1=Sugawara no Takami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619246}}. * Northern Lady '''Subsidiary Deities''' * {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|Ame no Hohi|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame no Hohi|1=Ame no Hohi}} - Ancestral deity of the Sugawara clan. * Great Ocean God (same as {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}) == History == === Foundation === In 901, following the exile of Sugawara no Michizane due to the {{ill|Shōtai Incident|ja|昌泰の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtai Incident|1=Shōtai Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511960}}, his son Takamasa, who was appointed Tosa no Gon no Kami, also moved to Tosa Province and resided in Ushioe. Takamasa's residence, once called "Kobata," now has a stone monument marking its location. The hill where his residence stood is named "Takami-yama" (officially called Saragamine) after Takamasa, and the name "Takami-cho" remains to this day. In Enki 3 (903), after Michizane's death, his sacred items (mirror, purple robe, and divine sword) were sent to Takamasa, who enshrined them, marking the shrine's founding. In Enki 5 (905), Michizane's retainer {{ill|Watarai Haruhiko|ja|渡会春彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Haruhiko|1=Shirafuji-no-Tayū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92665997}} (Shirotaifu) delivered these items but fell ill and died in Otsu Village, Tosa (now Otsu, Kochi City). His grave remains on Iwasaki Mountain (Otsu Kitaura) with a shrine called Shirotaifu Shrine. In Enki 6 (906), Takamasa returned to Kyoto, was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, and reinstated as head of the university. === Historical Overview === Since the era of the Chosokabe clan, the priesthood was held by the descendants of Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). The shrine was nearly abolished but was rebuilt in Keicho 11 (1606) by Masakatsu's son Miyaji Heibei with his private funds. In Meiji 6 (1873), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and significant renovations occurred during Michizane's millennium festival in Meiji 35 (1902). The main hall was destroyed during World War II air raids and later rebuilt. It is now a special shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located at the foot of Fudeyama, along the right bank of the {{ill|Kagami River|ja|鏡川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami River|1=Kagami River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15259987}}. The entrance is to the east, and the main hall faces east, though the approach bends in an S-shape. The Romon gate was built in Kaei 6 (1853) and restored in 1887. It is a three-bay, one-entrance hip-and-gable roof structure, designated as a tangible cultural property by Kochi City. The "Tenjin no Kusunoki" camphor tree, over 300 years old, is a natural monument. File:Ushioe-tenmangu honden.jpg|main hall File:Ushioe-tenmangu Haiden angle.jpg|Haiden File:Ushioe-tenmangu enclosure.jpg|Flying Plum Tree File:Ushioe-tenmangu Romon 2.jpg|Romon (Kochi City Cultural Property) == Subsidiary Shrines == * Shirotaifu Shrine ** deity: Watarai Haruhiko (Matsumoto Haruhiko). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Wakae Shrine ** deity: Spirits of Sugawara's attendants and Miyaji Masakatsu (Wakazaemon). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Sawara Shrine * Shimazaki Shrine ** deity: Shimazaki Michinaga (Michihisa). festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19 * Oyamazumi Shrine ** deity: Oyamatsumi. festivals: February 25, July 22, November 18-19. Monthly festival on the 20th of the lunar calendar. * Horyu Shrine ** deity: Dragon deity by the sacred pond. == Cultural Properties == === Kochi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Ushioe Tenmangu Romon (Building) - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). * Natural Monuments ** Tenjin-cho Camphor Tree - Designated on February 3, Showa 42 (1967). == location Information == '''location''' * Tenjin-cho 19-20, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture '''Access''' * Train: {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|en|Tosaden Kōtsū|WD=Q3537118|lt=Tosa Electric Railway|1=Tosaden Kōtsū}} Ino Line, get off at {{ill|Ōhashidōri Station|en|Ōhashidōri Station|WD=Q8081492|lt=Ohashi-dori Station|1=Ōhashidōri Station}}, 12-minute walk south. '''Surroundings''' * Fudeyama * Kagami River ** Tenjin Bridge - Originally named "Shinnyoji Bridge" when constructed during the Genwa era for the Yamanouchi family's mausoleum at Shinnyoji Temple. Renamed during the 8th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Toyoshige|1=Yamauchi Toyonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466475}}'s reign after Ushioe Tenmangu Shrine. * Shinnyoji Temple == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 'Kochi City History' (Kochi City Hall, 1920) * 'Family Tree' * Ryō Ōta 'Surname and Family History Dictionary' * 'The Illusion of Miyaji Shinsendo' (2014) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anraku-ji (Kochi)|ja|安楽寺 (高知市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anraku-ji (Kochi)|1=Anraku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450876}} (Kochi City) - Oku-no-in of the Shikoku 88 Temples. Traditionally founded by Sugawara Takamasa. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ushioe-tenmangu}} * [http://www.ushioe-tenmangu.jp/ Ushioe Tenmangu Official Website] * [http://www.webkochi.net/kanko/sanpo02.php Ushioe Tenmangu and Takami-yama] - [http://www.webkochi.net/ Web Kochi] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|潮江天満宮|version=102013910|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ushioe-tenmangu}} {{wikidata link|Q11565717|co|commons:Category:Ushioe-tenmangu|ja|潮江天満宮|fr|User:Immanuelle/Ushioe Tenmangu shrine}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
